top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize variability in cross-section dimensional accuracy between bars within same batch (IT8)
Increase ability to maintain grinding stock removal depth within +-0.005 mm across batch
Minimize batch-to-batch variability in surface hardness (HRC) across consecutive deliveries
Minimize likelihood of sole-source profile supplier dependency causing stoppages >5 days/year
Minimize likelihood of incoming profile batch quarantine from cross-section deviations >+-0.05 mm

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of profile geometry spec errors >+-0.005 mm causing die redesign
needs.importanceRationale: Die redesign costs EUR 5-15K and delays 6-12 weeks

needs.statement: Minimize time to receive quotation and first-article sample for non-standard profile geometry
needs.importanceRationale: 6-12 week tooling lead time for new geometry die

needs.statement: Increase ability to add new steel rail profile supplier to ASL within 8 weeks
needs.importanceRationale: Custom profile supplier qualification requires die trial + first-article dimensional approval

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of sole-source profile supplier dependency causing production line stoppages >5 days/year
needs.importanceRationale: Custom die = inherently sole-source; cannot switch without new die at new supplier

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of incoming profile batch cross-section deviations >+-0.05 mm triggering batch quarantine
needs.importanceRationale: IT8 tolerance (+-0.02 mm) with in-line SPC; quarantine threshold is 2.5x delivery tolerance

needs.statement: Increase ability to complete incoming dimensional inspection within 2 hours per tonne without specialist CMM
Minimize variability in cross-section dimensional accuracy between bars within same delivery batch (IT8 +-0.02 mm)
needs.importanceRationale: Cold drawing through fixed carbide die enforces geometric consistency bar-to-bar; SPC monitors every bar

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of surface hardness falling below 58 HRC at any contact-zone point
needs.importanceRationale: In-line IH with profile-matched coil; zone-selective depth control 0.5-5 mm

needs.statement: Increase ability to maintain grinding stock removal depth within +-0.005 mm across all bars in batch
needs.importanceRationale: Near-net-shape means minimal, uniform grinding stock; <0.3 mm stock removal vs 2-5 mm for bar stock

needs.statement: Increase ability to maintain grinding line utilization above 95% by eliminating rework from out-of-tolerance stock
needs.importanceRationale: 1% reject reduction = significant cost savings at high-volume guide rail producers

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of guide rail raceway centreline deviations from bar bow >0.2 mm/m causing rail rejection
needs.importanceRationale: Multi-roll straightening bank at ZOLLERN delivers <=0.2 mm/m as standard

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of premature grinding wheel wear from inconsistent surface hardness distribution
Increase ability to detect dimensional drift within first 5 bars of a new delivery batch
needs.importanceRationale: Early detection prevents propagation into active grinding line

needs.statement: Minimize batch-to-batch variability in surface hardness (HRC) across consecutive deliveries from same supplier
needs.importanceRationale: In-line IH with fixed coil geometry + automated quench; same machine, same parameters every batch

needs.statement: Minimize time and cost to recover from out-of-spec batch discovered after >50 bars in grinding
needs.importanceRationale: Recovery from a bad batch = scrapped grinding stock + rework + schedule disruption

needs.statement: Increase ability to maintain grinding parameter stability across consecutive batches without manual re-adjustment
Minimize likelihood of EN 10204 3.1 traceability gaps creating recall exposure for certified OEM customers
needs.importanceRationale: IATF 16949-certified; EN 10204 3.1 certs standard delivery; full heat-to-bar traceability

needs.statement: Minimize tooling NRE cost and lead time for new die during PPAP or prototype phase
needs.importanceRationale: EUR 5-15K die cost + 6-12 weeks; A2 (bar stock) requires zero tooling

needs.statement: Minimize profile bars rejected per batch from straightness deviations >0.2 mm/m after IH and roller straightening
Minimize likelihood of guide rail surface roughness Ra exceeding 1.6 um at pre-grind stage
